Disposition Analysis · Prepared for Feldberg Real Estate Holdings

1100 Old Ellis Road

A sale-leaseback and disposition strategy for a 28,818 SF multi-tenant office building in Roswell, and the realistic range to expect at sale.

01 / THE BUILDING

What You Own, And The Basis You Choose

Three tenants, 100% leased, two stabilized NNN through 2031. Start by choosing the expense basis: repairs is a real recurring cost and on by default, while reserves and management are buyer-elective layers.

The price at which 1100 ties a clean 6.5% NNN alternative returning an 8.79% unlevered IRR, held through the Boston Scientific rollover. This is the 8.0% column of the match matrix in Section 05; each $1.00/SF of leaseback rent moves it about $87K.

4Colorlaser pays NNN base rent plus its 24.05% pro-rata share of recoverable expenses, carried at the reassessed post-sale tax basis since the lease is signed at closing, plus its own metered utilities. At $18.48 / SF full-service on the basis you selected.

All three leases escalate 3% annually. Boston Scientific and DiVYO step on their August 1 lease anniversary; the 4Colorlaser leaseback is modeled on January 1 calendar bumps. The January 2027 model start carries each tenant at its current step and applies the next increase on its own anniversary.

Rentable area at 28,818 SF per the valuation basis. The 4Colorlaser leaseback space is carried at ~6,930 SF; a draft rent roll measured 7,130 SF. Confirm the remeasure before launch.

Owner-occupied, modeled as an assumed NNN leaseback at the rate set in Section 02. Each period ends 12/31 and steps 3%. Re-leases thereafter per the Section 05 assumptions.

Lease and rollover assumptions
  • Boston Scientific. In-place gross rent of $31.83/SF with 3% bumps each August 1. On expiry 7/31/2029 the model blends a 50% renewal probability: Boston Scientific renews at $24.50/SF NNN, or vacates and the space backfills at the rate set in Section 05, $21.50/SF NNN by default. The blended path carries 5 months of free rent after 9 months of downtime, $26/SF of tenant improvements, a 6% leasing commission, and 3% escalations. A one-time termination option exists for 7/31/2028 (notice by 12/31/2027, fee $102,555).
  • DiVYO Health. $16.07/SF NNN, stabilized. Two months free plus four months half-rent burn off around January 2027. 3% annual bumps each August 1, one five-year renewal at market, and a right of first offer on a sale.
  • 4Colorlaser. Owner-occupied, modeled as an assumed NNN lease on a five-year term with 3% escalations each January 1, at the rate you set above.
03 / THE PITCH TO BUYERS

The ROFO and the Listing Price

This sets the listing price: your in-place NOI capitalized across today's cap rate range, taken to market at an 8.25% cap that sits right inside the comp band. DiVYO Health holds a right of first offer on a sale, so this number also frames their first look.

The listing price · 8.25% cap$6,985,000or $242 / SF on in-place NOI

05 / THE TRUE RANGE

The Real Value: An Investor Won't Pay A Stabilized Cap Rate, And Here's Why

This is the section that matters. An income buyer underwrites the Boston Scientific rollover, not stabilized rent, and their real alternative is a clean single-tenant NNN deal.

THE TWO DIALS THAT SET THE FLOOR

Boston Scientific is 45% of the building. These two assumptions move the value more than anything else.

When Boston Scientific rolls in 2029, the model renews it at $24.50/SF NNN, or backfills the space at the rate you set below if it vacates. Set the backfill rate and the odds they renew, and every number recalculates live.

Backfill rate $/SF NNN
$18 conservative$25 market
Stabilized BSC rent $296,424 / yr
Renewal odds % renew
0% · vacatesrenews · 100%
≈ 9 mo downtime · 5 mo free · $26/SF TI
Investor floor at these settings $5.9M to $6.6M  ·  $206 to $229 / SF

  • 2.5% annual rent escalations
  • No vacancy, no re-leasing capital
  • The clean yield a 1031 buyer can get elsewhere today

~6.5–7.0%

THE RANGE TO EXPECT AT SALE
$206 to $229 / SF  ·  $5.9M to $6.6M
What an income buyer pays once the Boston Scientific rollover is priced in. The lower the renewal odds and backfill rate you defend, the deeper the discount to a stabilized value. This is the disclosed strike range.
06 / THE OWNER-USER FLOOR

Owner-User Value: The Backstop, Not The Primary Play Optional

Owner-user demand sets a practical floor, but the available space here is lower-level and sub-prime, so we treat this as a backstop rather than the lead market. Kept as an option.

Read on the backstop. The four size-comparable sales above (17K to 37K SF) cluster near $189/SF, which puts owner-user value around $5.5M on this building's 28,818 SF. That is the backstop if the investor pool stays thin, not the target. Four of eight shown; full set on request.
07 / THE GOAL

Sell The Cap Rate, Beat The Floor

Your goal is the highest price the building will bring, and our job is to get you there. The constraint is the investor's alternative, a clean NNN deal at 8.79%, which sets the floor a rational income buyer's return math will support. So we anchor at the ask, find the buyer who pays for the in-place cap rate and the upside, and beat that floor by as much as the deal allows. Every dollar above it is price won, not conceded.

The Listing Price · Our Ceiling
$6,985,000
$242 / SF at an 8.25% going-in cap
Anchored at the top of the comp band, on in-place NOI of $576,287. This is where we open.
Investor Reality · The Floor
$5.9M–6.6M
$206 to $229 / SF, IRR-matched
What a 1031 buyer's 8.79% return strictly supports after pricing the Boston Scientific rollover. A floor to beat, not a target to hit.
The spread~$0.4M to $1.1Mbetween the ask and the investor floor. Every dollar above it is price won, not conceded. Widens fast if renewal odds fall or the backfill rate is dialed back.
THE STRATEGY

Anchor at the ask, sell the cap rate, beat the floor

The investor-reality range is the floor, not the goal. We open at the $6,985,000 ask and sell the cap rate to the buyer who values it, beating the floor by as much as the deal allows. The upside case is a buyer who likes the deal and pays near the ask regardless of the 2029 rollover.

The levers that move the strike up the band: set the 4Colorlaser leaseback at a market rate, de-risk the Boston Scientific rollover before or during marketing, create competition among buyers, and use DiVYO's right of first offer as a stalking horse. The 2021 roof is already confirmed and baked into the floor. Investors lead; owner-users stay a backstop given the available space is sub-prime.

ADDENDUM

Ten-Year Cash Flow

The full hold behind the risk discussion, rolled up from the monthly engine on the buyer-underwritten basis, at the fixed base-case assumptions noted below. The Boston Scientific rollover lands in Years 3 to 4.

Open the 10-year cash flow
Net Operating Income Unlevered Cash Flow Negative cash flow year
$ per hold yearYr 12027Yr 22028Yr 32029Yr 42030Yr 52031Yr 62032Yr 72033Yr 82034Yr 92035Yr 102036
Net Operating Income$571,386$583,233$425,538$301,843$494,927$553,314$582,112$600,671$619,795$639,500
Leasing Capital (TI + LC)–––($447,791)––––––
HVAC, rooftop units($188,964)($33,394)–($18,712)($47,950)–($45,339)($105,855)––
Parking lot––($16,714)–––––($123,348)–
Unlevered Cash Flow$382,422$549,839$408,824($164,660)$446,977$553,314$536,773$494,816$496,447$639,500

Fixed base case, this chart does not move with the dials above. It holds Boston Scientific at a 50% renewal probability, renewing at $24.50/SF NNN or backfilling at $21.50/SF if the space vacates, blended to 9 months downtime, 5 months free, $26/SF TI, 6% commission, and 3% escalations, with the 4Colorlaser leaseback at $12.00/SF NNN. Operating expenses carry the post-sale tax reassessment to the buyer's basis. Year 4 is the only negative year, when Boston Scientific rolls and about $448K of re-leasing capital lands. With the 2021 roof outside the hold and the HVAC program spread across the years from an actual equipment inventory, building capital is about $580K in total. Operating costs escalate 1% to 2% by line, rents at 3%. Scroll horizontally to see all ten years.
